methods.description: Process for collecting undisturbed sediment cores of specific dimensions from four distinct study sites at a particular time for subsequent benthic diatom isolation.

steps.description: Undisturbed sediment cores (diameter: 5 cm; length: 10 cm) taken
steps.searchable_summary: collect undisturbed sediment cores of specified dimensions

steps.description: from four study sites (Figure 1) were used for benthic diatom isolation. Two sediment cores originated from the nature reserve "Heiligensee und Hütelmoor" northeast of Rostock, Mecklenburg Pomerania, Germany. The first core was taken on a sandy beach side (54.22550N, 12.17185E)... The second core was taken within the coastal peatland (54.21212N, 12.18343E)... The third sampling core was taken from the peatland Karrendorf (Figure 2C), in a small trench at 20-30 cm water depth (54.15796N, 13.38859E)... The fourth core was taken in the peatland Drammendorf ( Figure 2D) from a small trench at about 20-30 cm water depth (54.36971N, 13.24384E).
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from specified geographic locations
